title: Creamy Irish Potato Frittata
description: A nutty and fluffy Irish dish perfect for brunch, featuring potatoes and a creamy texture, made in a paella pan with all purpose flour and mantecare emulsification for a low sodium friendly meal.
instructions: Dust the paella pan with all purpose flour, shaking out excess.
In the paella pan, saute the diced potatoes and chopped onions in melted butter over medium heat until they are tender.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
In a large bowl, whisk together the beaten eggs, low fat milk, salt, and black pepper.
Pour the egg mixture over the potato mixture in the paella pan.
Transfer the paella pan to the preheated oven and bake for 25 minutes, until the eggs are set.
Remove the paella pan from the oven and sprinkle the chopped chives over the top.
Return the paella pan to the oven and bake for an additional 5 minutes, until the chives are lightly browned.
Remove the paella pan from the oven and let it cool for 5 minutes before slicing and serving.
